option.name: updatedContent
option.value: { "subject": "Cryptography", "topic": "Hashing Functions", "subtopic": "JWT Tokens", "views" : "0", "datePublished": "May 28, 2026", "dateUpdated" : "May 28, 2026", "title" : "JWT Tokens", "description" : "JSON Web Tokens (JWT) are a compact, URL-safe way to represent claims between parties. This note explains the JWT structure (header.payload.signature), how signatures are created using hashing (HMAC) or public-key cryptography (RSA/ECDSA), and common security pitfalls.", "objectives" : [ "Understand the three-part JWT structure and Base64URL encoding", "Explain how JWT signatures are created and verified (HMAC vs public-key)", "Identify common JWT security pitfalls (alg=none, weak secrets, missing claim validation)" ], "prerequisites" : [ "Basic understanding of hashing functions (e.g., SHA-256)", "Basic understanding of HTTP cookies and Authorization headers" ] }

option.name: updatedContent
